name: How to Create and Share a Google My Business Review Link
description: If you're a business owner, you know how important online reviews are for your business. Google My Business reviews can help your business stand out from the competition. But how do you create a review link that you can share with your customers?
main_title: How to Create and Share a Google My Business Review Link | Synup
richtext:
Google My Business reviews are a valuable tool to help grow your company. Using a Google Business review link can help you collect more quality customer feedback, which in turn boosts your search engine ranking. 

You can find your Google My Business review link one of three ways: 

  1. On your profile manager 
  2. With the Place ID finder 
  3. From Google search 

Choose a method to create and share a link below and then just follow the simple step-by-step guide to get more online reviews. 

Method One – Get the Review Link From Your Google Business Dashboard 

If you have an established Google Business profile that has already been verified, this is likely the easiest way to get a review link you can share online. 

Step 1: Go to Reviews from your Google Business dashboard 

‍

Go to your Google My Business dashboard (also called the Profile Manager) and click on the Reviews tab on the left side of the screen. 

Step 2: Click on Share Review Form 

‍

Click on the Share Review Form button inside the Get more reviews box. 

Step 3: Copy and Share the Generated Link 

Copy the link or share to your email, Facebook, Twitter, or WhatsApp profiles directly from the review link window. 

Method Two – Try the Place Finder ID Tool 

If your Google Business profile is not yet verified and you still want to get a review link, you can use the Place Finder ID tool. 

Step 1: Go to the Place Finder ID developer page and type in your location.   

‍

Go to the Place ID Finder developer page by clicking here: 

Place ID Finder Tool 

Then, enter your business name in the Enter a location box. 

Step 2: Copy the Place ID number.  

‍

The Place ID is the long number of letters and numbers that appears in a box over the location that you searched. Copy this number onto your clipboard. 

Step 3: Paste the Place ID into this code snippet. 

Paste the Place ID number into the code snippet below, replacing “place_id” at the end. 

https://search.google.com/local/writereview?placeid=<place_id>  becomes https://search.google.com/local/writereview?placeid=<ChIJHcib3_qAwokRyIzeqEpkVcc> 

You can now share this link to encourage user reviews. 

Method Three – Use Google Search 

Another simple way to create a Google Business review link is through the search function. 

Step 1: Search for your business on Google.  

Step 2: Click on your reviews. 

Step 3: Click Write a Review.  

Step 4: Copy and simplify the URL. 

Once you get to this step, copy the long URL in the address bar. It will look something like this: 

https://www.google.com/search?gs_ssp=eJzj4tVP1zc0TDIvNMs2SbMwYLRSMaiwsDA3t0iyNEo2TU42TDI1tTKoMDcySDYyMU2xNEk1SzIz9xItSCxIVMjKz8grVshKTM4uzs9TyM0HABZwFi0&q=papa+johns+jackson+mo&rlz=1C1PDZP_enUS1021US1021&oq=papa+johns&aqs=chrome.4.0i271j46i131i199i433i465i512j46i175i199i512j0i512j46i175i199i512l2j0i10i512l2j46i10i175i199i512j0i10i512.3443j0j7&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8#lrd=0x88778b92c5cc1b55:0x720c245d94e6b67,3,,,

Then, head over to a short link creator like Bit.ly or TinyURL to shorten the link. Then, it’s ready to share

‍
